original: Theodora crowned Empress of the Byzantine Empire
Vladislav II of Bohemia becomes king
Earliest record in Durham, England of 'Plough Monday', the first Monday after the Feast of Epiphany (Jan 6) when agricultural work could restart
Westmunster Church in Middelburg destroyed by heavy storm
Jacob van Neck's fleet leaves Bantam, Java in modern day Indonesia with 1 million pounds of pepper and cloves and a further half a ship full of nutmeg, mace and cinnamon

Most powerful earthquake in Italian recorded history strikes Sicily and Southern Italy with an estimated magnitude of 7.4. Tsunamis followed and devastated coastal villages on the Ionian Sea and the Straits of Messina.
Ferdinand VI of Spain & Pope Benedictus XIV sign concord, Spain recovers the right to appoint bishops and tax the clergy
Russian troops occupy Königsberg, East-Prussia [NS=Jan 22]
1st American life insurance company incorporated, Philadelphia
Francis Salvador becomes 1st Jew elected to office in America (SC)
Ching-Thang Khomba is crowned King of Manipur
Continental Congress convenes in NYC
Statisten & Vonckisten unite as Belgium
U.S. Marshal Robert Forsyth is killed in Augusta, Georgia, trying to serve court papers, first US marshal to die carrying out his duties
Earthquake at Martinique destroys half of Port Royal - 700 die
Alabama secedes from the Union (US Civil War)
Mexico City captured by Juarez (Lib) in War of Reform
Naval engagement near Galveston between CSS Alabama and USS Hatteras
Union forces capture Arkansas Post, or Fort Hindman, Arkansas
Charing Cross Station opens in London
Battle of Beverly, West Virginia, Confederate forces attack Union post
Steamship London sinks in storm off Land's End, England and kills 220
Benito Juárez becomes Mexican president again
1st livestock market newspaper published, Drover's Journal, Chicago
Hawaiian Historical Society is founded
William D. McCoy of Indiana appointed US minister to Liberia
Donghak Peasant Rebellion begins in Mujiang, Korea over local corruption, sparking the 1st Sino-Japanese War
Martha Hughes Cannon takes office as the 1st woman state senator in US, in Utah, after defeating her own husband
Bread & Roses Strike begins in Lawrence, Massachusetts following a pay cut
First sedan-type car, a Hudson, goes on display at the 13th Auto Show in NYC
Jacob Ruppert and Colonel Tillinghast Huston purchase the New York Yankees for $460,000, Ruppert pays his portion in cash
Austrian forces capture Mount Lovćen, Serbia. Serbian army retreats, evacuating to Greek island of Corfu
Guy Bolton & PG Wodehouse's musical "Have a Heart" premieres in New York
3-year-old German communist party (Spartacus) crushed
Romania annexes Transylvania
French passenger ship Afrique sinks near La Rochelle; 553 die
1st Dutch Dada-evening (Theo Van Doesburg & Kurt Schwitters)
French & Belgian troops occupy Ruhr to collect reparations
A republic is proclaimed in Greece; King George II is deposed and Eleutherios Venizelo is named Prime Minister of the Greek National Assembly
Royale Theater (Golden, CBS Radio Playhouse) opens at 242 W 45th NYC
Charles Anderson enters Kentucky House of Representatives
Frances Moulton is elected as the first female president of a US national bank
Princess Irene Brigade established in Congleton
-23°F (-31°C), Kingston, Rhode Island (state record)
Japan conquers Kuala Lumpur, Malaya
US & Britain relinquish extraterritorial rights in China
Crakow-Plaszow Concentration Camp established
Bert Bell becomes second NFL commissioner, moves Chicago headquarters to Philadelphia
Bollingen Prize for poetry awarded to Marianne Moore
Two ton locomotive swept into ravine by an avalanche in Austria, killing 10
American plane mass-murderer Jack Gilbert Graham is executed via the Gas Chamber
The African Convention is founded in Dakar
Bollingen Prize for poetry awarded to Theodore Roethke
LaMar Clark sets pro boxing record of 44 consecutive knockouts
Racial riot at University of Georgia
Landslide at dormant volcano Huascaran in Peru kills an estimated 4,000
Beatles release single "Please Please Me" / "Ask Me Why"; peaks at #2 in UK, and #3 in US
First government report by US Surgeon General Luther Terry warning that smoking may be hazardous
Panama ends diplomatic relations with US
The Beatles single "I Want to Hold Your Hand" reaches #80 in US (Cashbox)
"Daktari" African adventure series premieres on CBS TV
550 die in landslides in mountains behind Rio de Janeiro after rain
Romeinse Curie installs Council for Pontifical Study commission
San Diego is granted an NBA franchise to be called the Rockets; franchise moves to Houston for the 1971-72 season
Explorer 36 (GEOS-B) launched into earth orbit (1080/1570 km)
"Hooked on a Feeling" by BJ Thomas peaks at #5
Jethro Tull's "This Was Jethro Tull" album debuts
Irish republican political party Sinn Féin party splits between those in favor of abstentionism (of not taking any seats won in the parliaments) and those where against
Super Bowl IV, Tulane Stadium, New Orleans, LA: KC Chiefs beat Minnesota Vikings, 23-7; MVP: Len Dawson, Kansas City, QB
Tigers ace reliever John Hiller, 27, sufferes a heart attack, but later makes a remarkable comeback to record 38 saves
American League adopts designated hitter rule
Famous Australian cricket victory at the SCG - Pakistan chasing 158 is all out for 106
ABC airs final episode of "Love, American Style"
Soviet Soyuz 17 carries 2 cosmonauts to space station Salyut 4
Military coup in Ecuador, President Guillermo Lara leaves
France releases Abu Daoud, a Palestinian suspected of involvement in massacre of Israeli athletes at 1972 Munich Olympics
MLB Chicago Cubs trade outfielder Rick Monday to Los Angeles Dodgers for Bill Buckner
Poetry's Bollingen Prize awarded to David Ignatow
Soyuz 27 links with Salyut 6 & Soyuz 26 (1st time 3 spacecraft link)
British team led by Ranulph Fiennes completes longest & fastest crossing of Antarctica, reaching Scott base after 75 days (2,500 miles)
South Pacific island of Palau adopts constitution
Atlanta, Georgia's temperature goes below zero F
Honduras adopts constitution
Edmonton’s Pat Hughes sets NHL record for the fastest 2 shorthanded goals by one player at just 25 seconds apart in a 7-5 win at St. Louis; first player to break a Wayne Gretzky record
Denver Nuggets 163, San Antonio Spurs 155-highest-scoring NBA game
NASA space shuttle STS 41-B moves to launch pad
Supreme Court reinstated $10M award to Karen Silkwood's family
CDC MMWR publishes guidelines for screening U.S. blood supply for AIDS antibodies with ELISA test that would be available later that year
Douglas Wilder of Virginia, 1st African American Lieutenant Governor since reconstruction sworn in
Largest crowd (76,633) at NFL NY Giant Stadium (beat Wash 17-0)
ABC premiere of fact-based "Evil in Clear River"
USSR announces it will participate in Seoul Summer Olympics
140 nations agree to ban chemical weapons (poison gas, etc)
Denver Nuggets' rookie Jerome Lane misses 7 free throws in a game against Milwaukee, one missed by 2 feet
Kindergarten student caught with loaded handgun at Bronx school
200,000 demand return of Lithuania's independence
Actor Joseph Cotton (84) undergoes cancer operation to remove his larynx
Bob Knight becomes college basketball's Big 10 winningest coach with his 229th victory
Ben Johnson's 1st race after being stripped of his 1988 Olympic Gold medal for steroid use, he finishes 2nd
Congress empowers Bush to order attack on Iraq
Soviets storm buildings in Vilnius to block Lithuania independence
Algeria's President Chadli announces his resignation
British Airways ends one of the longest libel actions in its history
Howard Stern Radio Show begins transmitting to Buffalo, New York on  WKBW
Hyderabad score 6 for 944 against Andhra Pradesh in Ranji Trophy
Irish government announces end of a 20-year broadcasting ban on IRA
5th TV network (WB) Warner Brothers begins broadcasting
Birmingham Barracudas (based in Alabama) granted CFL franchise, experiment only lasts one season
DC-9 crashes near Maria La Baya, Colombia: 51 die, 9 yr old girl lives
NHLPA & owners agree to end NHL strike
Haiti becomes a member of the Berne Convention copyright treaty
Space Shuttle STS 72 (Endeavour 10), launches into space
Satellite Telstar 401 fails
Sidi-Hamed massacre takes place in Algeria, over 100 people are killed.

New York Knicks hold an opponent under 100 points for NBA-record 29th straight game in a 76-75 loss to the Houston Rockets; streak snaps 46-year-old NBA record of 28 straight set by the Fort Wayne Pistons in 1954-55
Washington Capitals' German goaltender Olaf Kölzig records his 32nd NHL career shutout in a 1-0 win against the visiting Edmonton Oilers
29 people are killed and 12 are injured after a bus veers off a mountain road in Doti, Nepal
46 people are killed and 12 are missing after a landslide buries a village in the Yunnan province, China
Pianist Menahem Presser (90) makes his Berlin Philharmonic debut as soloist in Mozart's 17th Piano Concerto; he had fled Nazi persecution in Germany as a teenager
Kolinda Grabar-Kitarović is elected as the first female President of Croatia
In China's first significant #MeToo moment Beihang University in Beijing fires a professor for sexual misconduct
US PresidentDonald Trumpcauses worldwide controversy when it is reported he called African countries "shitholes" during imigration meeting
Diego the giant 100-year-old tortoise retires to the Galapagos islands after his high libido is credited with saving his species
Ireland becomes the country with the world's highest COVID-19 infection rate after a dramatic surge results in seven-day rolling average of 1,394 cases per million
The king of Malaysia, Al-Sultan Abdullah declares a state of emergency over COVID-19 till 1 August, suspending parliament and giving the government new powers
Quebec announces it will impose a healthcare tax on unvaccinated adults who are accounting for 50% of ICU cases (scrapped Feb 2022)
WHO warns more than half of Europe could catch COVID-19 within weeks, warning of a "west-to-east tidal wave"
Death toll from Zambia's cholera outbreak reaches 300 as the president urges people to move away from dense urban areas
Huge ancient city discovered in the Amazon, in Upano area of eastern Ecuador, capable of supporting 10,000 people with sophisticated roads and canals
Largest dam removal in US history enters crucial phase as reservoir on Iron Gate Dam lowered in Klamath River through California and Oregon
More than 300 people have died in flooding caused by the highest levels of River Congo in 60 years, especially in Kinshasa, DR Congo
New study finds largest great ape, Gigantopithecus blacki at 10 feet tall (3 meters), from southern China, went extinct because of climate change between 215,000 and 295,000 years ago
Rare copy of 'mint' first issue of "The Amazing Spider-Man" sells for $1.38 million and "Superman #1", goes for $2.34m at auction
South Africa begins its case accusing Israel of genocidal acts against Palestinians in Gaza and violating the 1948 genocide convention at the International Court of Justice in The Hague
The US and UK launch multiple air-strikes against Iranian-backed Houthis in Yemen in retaliation for attacks on vessels in the Red Sea
Holy Roman EmperorMaximilian IIgrants Austrian nobility freedom of religion
Colley Cibber's play "Rival Fools" premieres in London
Frisia bansVoltaire's book "Traité sur la tolérance" (Treatise on Tolerance) in which he called for religious toleration and targeted religious fanaticism, especially that of the Jesuits
French astronomerCharles Messieradds M51 (spiral galaxy in Canes Venatici) to his catalog
Titania and Oberon, moons of Uranus, discovered byWilliam Herschel
First public demonstration of telegraph messages sent using dots and dashes at Speedwell Ironworks in Morristown, New Jersey bySamuel Morseand Alfred Vail
Henrik Ibsen's play "Vildauden," (The Wild Duck) premieres in Oslo
Jaap Edenskates world record 1500m (2:35)
Insulin is first used on humans whenFrederick Bantinginjects 14-year-old Canadian Leonard Thompson to treat his diabetes
Frank Kelloggreplaces Charles Hughes as US Secretary of State
Louis B. Mayerhead of film studio MGM announces creation of Academy of Motion Picture Arts and Sciences
Amelia Earhartflies from Honolulu to Oakland, California (non-stop, of course)
Sergei Prokofiev's ballet "Romeo & Juliet" premieres in Leningrad
Enver Hoxhadeclares People's Republic of Albania with himself dictator
J. Edgar Hooverdeclines 6 figure offer to become president of International Boxing Club
Pakistani cricket batsmanHanif Mohammadsmashes 499 for Karachi v Bahawalpur, then 1st class world record
SF Warriors centerWilt Chamberlainscores 67 points in 134-129 defeat to LA Lakers at Cow Palace
Billy Martinnamed NY Yankee manager for 3rd time
LA Kings' centerMarcel Dionnepicks up his 24th NHL career hat-trick and adds 3 assists for 6 points in a 9-7 win at Washington; Kings' first road win after 8 straight losses

American singer-songwriterPaul Simonbrings his “Born at the Right Time” tour to South Africa, at Ellis Park Stadium in Johannesburg
Independent presidential candidateRoss Perotpublicly returns to politics
American comedianJon Stewartsucceeds Craig Kilborn as host of late-night talk and satirical news program "The Daily Show" on Comedy Central in US
Bülent Ecevitbecomes Prime Minister of Turkey for the 4th time
Marty Schottenheimerresigns as head coach of the Kansas City Chiefs

Washington right wingJaromír Jágrscores his 10th career hat-trick and adds 4 assists for 7 points and his 1,200th NHL point in a 12-2 win against Florida; Jágr has 20+ goals in each of his 13 NHL seasons
AuthorJ. K. Rowlingfinishes the 7th and last Harry Potter novel in room 552 of the Balmoral Hotel, Edinburgh

John Du Prez and Eric Idle's musical "Spamalot", based on the film "Monty Pythonand the Holy Grail," and starring Tim Curry and Davis Hyde Pierce, closes at the Shubert Theatre, NYC, after 1,575 and 3 Tony Award wins

During a concert in Maui,Mick Fleetwoodannounces thatChristine McViewill be rejoiningFleetwood Mac

After 24 seasons and 6 Super Bowl titles, coachBill Belichickand the NFL New England Patriots part ways; Patriots finished season in last place in AFC East at 4-13
First recorded lottery in England is drawn in St Paul's Cathedral in London. First prize was £5,000, other prizes included silver plate, tapestries and high quality linen cloth.
